meta.title: Erféro Keoula — Freelance Shopify Developer | Custom Shopify Store Design
meta.description: Expert freelance Shopify developer (5+ years of experience): custom Shopify store builds, redesigns, conversion rate optimization (CRO) and SEO. 200+ stores delivered, ready to sell in days.

hero.badge: Freelance Shopify Developer · Open for new projects
hero.subtitle: I design and build fast, custom Shopify stores made to convert — for brands that refuse the generic theme.

painPoints.title: Your store deserves better than a generic theme
painPoints.subtitle: Most online stores lose sales to problems that are invisible to the naked eye. Here are the most common ones.

items.title: A conversion rate that won't budge
items.description: The average Shopify store conversion rate sits around 1.4%. You've got traffic, maybe even great products — but sales aren't following. A generic design, a confusing checkout flow, or unclear product presentation is enough to push away visitors who were already reaching for their card.

items.title: Almost 1 in 2 carts abandoned
items.description: On average, 70% of visitors abandon their cart before paying. Shipping costs revealed too late, forced account creation, overly long forms, missing payment methods... every extra bit of friction in the checkout flow is a lost sale, often just a few clicks from closing.

items.title: A slow site that loses mobile shoppers
items.description: Most Shopify traffic comes from mobile. If your store takes more than 3 seconds to load on a phone, a good chunk of your visitors leave before even seeing a product — and Google also penalizes slow sites in its rankings, costing you traffic on top of sales.

items.title: A design that doesn't build trust
items.description: A theme that looks like a thousand other stores, poorly framed product photos, a layout that breaks on mobile... Visitors judge a site's credibility within seconds, often before reading a single word. Without that layer of trust, even the best product in the world won't convert.

items.title: Apps that slow everything down
items.description: An app for reviews, another for pop-ups, a third for upsells... each one adds code and requests that pile up. The result: a slower site, sometimes broken layouts, and an experience that breaks instead of sells — the opposite of what installing them was supposed to achieve.

items.title: Invisible on Google
items.description: Without a clean technical structure (tags, speed, internal linking) or content actually built to answer your customers' searches, your store stays invisible on Google — even with great products. The result: you depend 100% on paid ads to drive traffic, which eats into your margin over time.

services.title: Not a free theme. A proven sales structure.
services.subtitle: My specialty is the premium Story theme — but I also build on default templates or from scratch, depending on your budget and needs. And more importantly, I replicate the exact mechanics used by DTC brands that actually sell: immediate social proof, urgency funnels, objection-killing FAQs. Here's concretely what that changes for you.

items.title: A page structure that's already sold thousands of units
items.description: High-impact hero → immediate social proof → benefits → objections defused → guarantee → purchase. I've seen this cascade work across dozens of stores — I'm not reinventing the wheel, I'm executing it cleanly for your brand.

items.title: Social proof sections that build trust at a glance
items.description: Trustpilot-style review carousels, happy-customer counters, press logos — the same trust levers used by DTC brands that are actually thriving, integrated cleanly instead of slapped on.

items.title: Urgency funnels that grow order value without feeling pushy
items.description: Countdown timers, bundle offers ("buy 2, get 50% off"), tiered packs — built to drive immediate purchases without coming across as aggressive or low-cost.

items.title: Comparison tables that answer objections before they're raised
items.description: "Us vs. the alternatives," before/after, product comparisons — turning a visitor's hesitation into a purchase decision while they're still on the page.

items.title: An FAQ built objection by objection, not copy-pasted
items.description: Every question answers a real hesitation specific to your market (safety, efficacy, shipping, guarantees) — not a generic FAQ recycled from a Shopify tutorial.

items.title: Mobile-first, real speed — not just "responsive"
items.description: Most of your customers buy from their phone. Every section is designed, tested and optimized to load fast on mobile — not just resized as an afterthought.

items.title: Any theme, your call
items.description: Story by default if you give me full creative freedom, but also standard Shopify templates, a third-party theme of your choice, or a fully custom-built theme — your budget and needs decide, not me.

items.title: Full store management, like a real assistant
items.description: Adding products from A to Z, optimized listings, sales monitoring, ongoing advice to keep improving — beyond the build itself, I can run your store day to day.

pricing.title: Clear packs, starting at €100
pricing.subtitle: Price depends on the theme and project scope — here's a starting point, to refine together based on your exact needs.

faq.title: Everything you're wondering before reaching out

items.q: How much does a Shopify store cost?
items.a: It depends on the project's complexity (number of pages, features, custom design or not). After our discovery call, you'll get a clear, detailed quote — no surprises.

items.q: How long does a project take?
items.a: On average 3 days for a complete store, depending on complexity and how fast feedback comes back. You get a precise timeline upfront.

items.q: I don't have products or a logo yet, is that a problem?
items.a: Not at all. We can move in parallel: I build the structure while you finalize your branding, or I can help with that too.

items.q: Do you work with clients outside Benin?
items.a: Yes, I work remotely with clients worldwide, in both French and English.

items.q: Do I fully own my store?
items.a: Completely. Your Shopify store is entirely yours, along with any custom code built for you.
I stay available for maintenance, improvements or urgent fixes, with or without an ongoing support plan depending on your needs.

items.q: Can you improve an existing store?
items.a: Yes, auditing and redesigning existing stores is one of my most requested services.
